# Notebook

TODO bugs

Codebase structure

- [x] use separate quarto docs in /report for: abstract, background, methods, results, discussion, supplement
- [x] combine in a full quarto manuscript using _includes
- [ ] Update CLAUDE.md to reflect structural changes

Code functionality

- [x] check results pipeline runs
- [ ] check the figures against rev comments
- [x] fix variant code and data issues

Reporting

- [ ] Add mathematical notation for the model in the main text, with explanation
- [ ] create paragraph-by-paragraph one line summary OF THE CURRENT TEXT in a new section of `Research-narrative.md`
- [ ] write an abstract based on Research-narrative.qmd
- [ ] assess whether the flow of text matches with intended research narrative

Admin

- [ ] flag remaining reviewer comments and suggest actions
- [ ] refactor inline referencing with zotero citation keys (https://www.zotero.org/kathsherratt/library)

TODO features

- [x] normalise by population before scoring #pr-148
- [x] remove geographic specificity as an exposure, add geographic specificity as -confounder-
- [ ] add choice of one OR both case/death as -counfounder- (rationale: not independent; "were modellers for death targets also accounting for cases")
- [ ] create a DAG to represent modelling approach, include in supplement
- [ ] use a schematic in the main text to demonstrate approach

Future work (See: future-work.qmd)

- [ ] expand to cover US
- [ ] repeated measures stats (ICC)
- [ ] compare the text against the STROBE reporting guidelines and flag issues: https://resources.equator-network.org/reporting-guidelines/strobe/#summary

## notes

### 2 April 2026

- are there any examples of simpson's paradox where the ensemble changes direction from all component forecasts?
- forecast trend vs observed trend / rate of change (aka log)
- Cramer distance among models
- predicting performance of ensemble based on size; bias-variance
- What's driving the WIS?
  Exploring the WIS as a composite score
- use model contributors...
  - orcID or author lookup, volume of publications/citations, as indicator of ... expertise
  - number per team as indicator of resources
